query: I want to get the exact on-disk size of mlx-community/Qwen3-Next-80B-A3B-Instruct-4bit without touching my real HF caches. Use hf download --dry-run with a throwaway temp directory, parse the reported total, and tell me: (a) the full repo size, (b) whether it fits in a 45 GB remaining-space budget, and (c) the recommended shell command to actually download it to /data/hf-cache afterwards.

The Nemotron-3-Nano-30B model card has a comparison table showing GPT-OSS-20B column values for MMLU-Pro, AIME25, GPQA, SWE-Bench, TauBench, and RULER. The openai/gpt-oss-20b page itself shows MMLU-Pro, GPQA Diamond, SWE-bench Verified, HLE, and LEXam scores. Build a unified comparison table with both sources, mark which values came from the Nemotron page vs the GPT-OSS page, keep exact benchmark names without normalization, and note any score discrepancies between the two sources.

The model card for nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Nano-30B-A3B-BF16 has a benchmark table. Extract all rows and values from it exactly as written — do not normalize metric names like MMLU-Pro or BFCL v4. Then tell me which metrics are unique to this page and which overlap with what is on the openai/gpt-oss-20b page.

I have MMLU=78.3 from the Nemotron model card and MMLU-Pro=73.6 from the gpt-oss-20b page. Are these the same benchmark? Should I merge them into one column in my comparison table or keep them separate? Also explain the difference between MMLU, MMLU-Pro, and MMLU (CoT).

I downloaded mlx-community/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-OptiQ-4bit to /data/hf-cache last week but I am not sure if the download completed. How do I verify the cache is complete and not metadata-only, and what exact hf command tells me which files are missing?

I have a list of 46 Hugging Face model repos I want to download. Some are from mlx-community, some from lmstudio-community. What environment variables should I set for a reliable batch download to /data/hf-cache, and what is the correct shell pattern that handles HF_HOME, timeouts, and safe quoting of repo IDs?

My model-inventory.md Markdown note has a table where the HF ID column contains backtick-wrapped repo IDs like `mlx-community/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit`. Convert every ID in that column into a Markdown inline link to the HF model page. Footnote-style references must not be used because many Markdown renderers do not handle them inside table cells.

I keep two persistent HF caches: default at /data/hf-default and external at /mnt/models/hf. I want a small JSON config file that registers both by name so my workflows can reference them. What should the schema look like, and should I include my temporary dry-run cache in the same file?

In my model inventory note I list three HF cache homes: laptop, nas, and external-ssd. Check whether mlx-community/gemma-4-26B-A4B-it-qat-8bit is usable in each home. Do not assume there are only two caches, and explain where each cache path came from.

hf download LiquidAI/LFM2.5-8B-A1B-MLX-bf16 says the model is complete, but I still see a bunch of .incomplete files in the cache. Is the model actually broken? Check it by following the snapshot symlinks, not just by counting .incomplete files, and tell me which files are safe cleanup residue.

I have the same HF repo in several cache homes and want to delete duplicate copies from one drive. Build a per-cache matrix with usable/metadata-only/incomplete status, actual du size, and only generate rm -rf commands for copies where another cache has a usable snapshot-linked copy.
Fine-tune this Llama model with LoRA on my support dataset and give me the training script.
Explain what GPQA Diamond measures and whether it is a good benchmark for reasoning models.
Install transformers, accelerate, and bitsandbytes into my Python virtualenv.
Summarize this PDF and extract the tables into CSV.
Deploy a Text Generation Inference server for Mistral on Kubernetes.
I need a PowerPoint deck comparing AI coding tools for our leadership team.
Debug why this React table component rerenders too often when I sort columns.
Download this YouTube transcript and summarize it.
